Description: The first <button> contains only an <svg> with no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visually-hidden text. Screen readers announce it as an empty button, making it impossible for assistive-technology users to understand its purpose.
Recommendation: Add aria-label="<purpose>" to the <button> or include a <span className="sr-only"> with descriptive text.

Description: The second <button> also contains only an <svg> without any accessible label. Same impact as the first icon button.
Recommendation: Add aria-label="<purpose>" to the <button> or include a <span className="sr-only"> with descriptive text.

Description: The <svg> elements lack role="img" and a <title> child element. Without these, assistive technologies cannot convey the graphic's meaning.
Recommendation: Add role="img" and a <title> element inside each <svg>, or mark decorative SVGs with aria-hidden="true".

Description: The <nav> element has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. When a page has multiple nav landmarks, users cannot distinguish them.
Recommendation: Add aria-label="Main navigation" or similar to the <nav> element.

Description: The <section> has no aria-labelledby linking it to the <h2>. While the heading is present, explicitly associating it improves landmark navigation.
Recommendation: Add an id to the <h2> and aria-labelledby on the <section>, e.g. <section aria-labelledby="overview-heading">.

Description: Reading offsetHeight (line 12) forces a synchronous reflow, then immediately writing style.minHeight (line 13) triggers a second reflow. This read-write cycle in a single frame causes layout thrashing and jank.
Recommendation: Batch DOM reads and writes separately using requestAnimationFrame, or use ResizeObserver to react to size changes without forced reflows.

Description: setTimeout injects items after 500ms without any placeholder or reserved space. This shifts existing content downward, increasing Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS), a Core Web Vital.
Recommendation: Reserve space with a skeleton/placeholder of known dimensions, or load data before initial render to avoid layout shift.

Description: The for promo.png is below the fold but lacks loading="lazy". The browser will fetch it eagerly even if not in the viewport, wasting bandwidth and delaying LCP.
Recommendation: Add loading="lazy" to below-fold images: .

Issue: Image missing explicit width and height
Description: The tag has no width/height attributes or aspect-ratio CSS. The browser cannot reserve space before the image loads, contributing to CLS.
Recommendation: Add explicit width and height attributes (e.g. width="800" height="400") or use CSS aspect-ratio.

Issue: List items rendered without stable keys
Description: items.map() does not provide a key prop. React falls back to array index, which causes unnecessary re-renders and DOM reconciliation overhead when the list changes.
Recommendation: Provide a stable, unique key: items.map((item) => <div key={item} ...>).

Description: The useEffect with an empty dependency array runs layout-affecting code on every mount. Combined with setTimeout, this guarantees a visible layout shift on every page load.
Recommendation: Consider server-side or static data fetching (e.g. getServerSideProps / loader) to have data ready before first paint.

Issue: Dynamic list items lack ARIA live region
Description: Items injected asynchronously are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology receive no notification that content has appeared.
Recommendation: Wrap the list container with aria-live="polite" and optionally aria-atomic="true" so assistive technologies announce new content.
